_comment: expected ∈ skills ∪ {none}. Every language positive doubles as a negative for the other four (cross-language confusion is measured by the matrix). 'none' queries are near-misses: they share vocabulary with type-safety work but should fire no vibe-types skill. Keep these realistic — concrete detail, casual phrasing, occasional typos — and prefer implicit language signals over naming the language.

queries.query: im getting `cannot borrow `config` as mutable more than once at a time` in my actix handler. how do i restructure this so it compiles

I have an Order struct with a status: String and a pile of Option<> fields for each state (shipped_at, cancelled_at, refunded_at). feels wrong — how do i model this so invalid combos just don't compile
whats the cleanest way to make a UserId and a ProductId that can't be passed in for each other but are both just u64 underneath
i want a builder for my http client where calling .send() before you've set the url is a compile error, not a runtime panic. typestate?
right now i have a catch-all `_ => {}` in my match over this enum. how do i make it so adding a new variant later forces me to handle it everywhere instead of silently falling through
is PhantomData the right way to tag my Meters vs Feet wrapper types at the type level so i can't add them together
i need Send + Sync on my shared cache so the compiler stops me if i accidentally stick an Rc<RefCell<>> in there. how do i set that up with Arc
my parser returns a bare bool for 'is this a valid token' and then i re-check downstream. how do i carry the proof in a type instead of validating twice
mypy keeps flagging that my get_user() returns Optional[User] but the caller treats it as User. how do i narrow it properly instead of asserting not None everywhere
i've got a big settings dict and i want the keys and value types checked statically instead of KeyError-ing at runtime. is TypedDict the move here
how do i tell pyright that this argument just needs to have a .read() method, without forcing it to inherit from some base class
trying to make a frozen @dataclass for my domain model but i still need one lazily-computed cached field. whats the idiomatic way
i keep accidentally passing raw strings where an email is expected. want a distinct Email type so that's a type error — NewType?
my @retry decorator erases the wrapped function's signature and the type checker loses the params. ParamSpec to preserve them?
how can i restrict the `order` param to only the literal strings 'asc' or 'desc' so a typo becomes a type error
got this untyped module with a bunch of *args/**kwargs helpers. add annotations so it passes mypy --strict
my given instance for Show[Order] isn't being found at the call site. how does given/using resolution actually work and how do i debug it
i want an opaque type AccountId over Long with a smart constructor that validates, but zero runtime wrapping cost. how do i do that
modeling a payment domain with a sealed trait and case classes, and i want exhaustive matching. is this the idiomatic ADT approach now
can i just use a union type Int | String here instead of Either, and how does the compiler narrow it in a match
trying to add an extension method on String but the compiler can't resolve it from another package. what import do i need
how do context functions with ?=> work for threading a Connection through a bunch of calls without passing it explicitly every time
after refactoring my ADT to the new enum syntax i'm getting 'value is not a member of' on what used to be a case class field. whats going on
want to auto-derive a JsonCodec for my case class with `derives` instead of writing it by hand
my lake build fails with `unsolved goals` in a theorem about list append associativity. how do i actually close this proof
how do i define a vector type indexed by its length so that calling head on an empty one is a type error rather than a runtime check
i have a structure with an invariant expressed as a Prop field. how do i construct a value of it — do i need to supply a proof term
the termination checker rejects my recursion over a tree. how do i give it a termination_by / decreasing_by so it accepts it
when should i reach for simp vs omega vs decide to discharge a goal like this one about nat inequalities
i want to prove that my insert function keeps the list sorted, with the invariant encoded in the type so the compiler checks it
my instance for a custom type class isn't getting picked up during elaboration. how does instance resolution work in lean 4
model a door state machine where an illegal transition (opening an already-open door) is simply not provable, using an inductive relation
tsc isn't narrowing my discriminated union — i switch on the `kind` field but it still thinks the value could be the other variant inside the case
i want a branded UserId type so a plain string can't be assigned to it by accident. how do i brand it
how do i write a mapped type that takes Config and makes every field readonly and optional in one shot
can i use a template literal type to constrain route strings to a pattern like `/users/:id` at compile time
my zod schema and my hand-written type keep drifting apart. how do i derive the static type from the schema so there's one source of truth
make this get() helper generic so the return type follows from keyof the object you pass in, instead of being any
i want an exhaustiveness check on this switch so that adding a new union member becomes a compile error. the never assignment trick?
getting `Type 'string | undefined' is not assignable to type 'string'` off process.env.PORT with strict null checks on. how do i handle it cleanly
